question: Read: 'Increasingly, smart devices use a digital fingerprint or facial recognition software rather than a PIN.' A PIN in this context is ________.
choices: a personal identification number used as a security code

question: Read: 'You'll have several remote devices that are either wearables or actually embedded in your body to alert your doctor to any medical problems.' The purpose of these devices is to ________.
choices: connect you to the internet at all times
notify your doctor about medical problems

question: Read: 'When we last heard, the mirror was still being developed in a lab in France.' This uses which passive tense?
Read: 'A transmitter is worn on the body.' This is an example of the ________ passive.
In the passive grammar section, we can use ________ or 'they' to refer to people in general instead of the passive in informal language.
In the passive letter exercise, choose the correct passive: 'Secondly, the watch clearly ________ before being sent from your factory.' (not/check)
Read: 'There was a dramatic increase in the number of users.' → 'The number of users ________.'
In the trend description, 'There has been a ________ increase in the numbers of IoT devices used globally' — what word replaces 'steady rise'?
Which phrase correctly describes a downward trend?
choices: The number of users rose steadily
There has been a steady growth

question: In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', what happens to a new device eventually, even if you are an early adopter?
In the vocabulary crossword, the clue 'Small problems or faults that prevent something from working well' matches the word ________.
In the vocabulary crossword, 'Make a copy of information held on a computer or other device' matches the phrase ________.
Read: 'If you switch brands of gaming console, none of your old games will work.' This is an example of a ________ issue.
Read from the VR article: 'This is a description of what it might feel like for some people with ASD to go to a shopping centre.' What is ASD?
choices: A type of advanced sensory device

question: Read from the VR article: 'Virtual Reality is a computer-generated simulation of an experience, which is seen in 3D, and which can feel almost exactly like really being in that situation.' VR is described as a ________.
Read from the VR article: 'So far, it has had six million views.' What had six million views?
choices: The National Autistic Society's VR autism experience video

question: In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'The noise was too much for her and she finished by running from the room' should be replaced by ________.
In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'You can't expect to keep a job if you're always late!' should be replaced by ________.

Read: 'People started taking selfies to manage and curate what they wanted to tell the world about their physical attributes, their personality, their relationships and their hobbies.' What does 'manage and curate' suggest?
choices: People share everything about their lives spontaneously
People carefully control and select their online self-presentation
People rely on technology to manage their social lives
People use social media to manage their professional careers

question: Read: 'Some people don't want photos of themselves to look like selfies.' Which of these is stated as a FACT (not an opinion) in the selfies section?
choices: Some people don't want photos of themselves to look like selfies
It's fashionable to employ a local photographer to capture perfect images
Young people reject most of the photos which they take of themselves
People who care about their image don't worry how much they spend

question: Read: 'You can add filters to enhance how you look, remove any flaws or even change the shape or size of your facial features.' Which of these is an example of 'enhancing' a photo?
choices: Posting the photo immediately without editing
Adding filters to improve your appearance
Deleting the photo from your phone
Sharing the photo with only close friends

question: The word 'influential' means ________.
choices: able to change what people do or think
wanting something that someone else has

question: The word 'endless' means ________.
choices: able to change what people think
in large quantities or for a very long time

question: Read: 'It was once believed that guided missiles would be used to deliver post.' Which impersonal passive pattern is used?
In the grammar exercises, 'Over half a million passwords are thought ________ stolen in the hack.'
In the grammar exercises, 'It has often been ________ that the world was a simpler place before people had telephones.'
Read from the Bell article: 'Today, it is estimated that there are more phones in the world than people!' This uses which impersonal passive pattern?
In the for-and-against essay structure, the INTRODUCTION should ________.
choices: give all the arguments in favour and against
state your opinion clearly from the start
summarise the topic and outline what will be discussed
focus only on one side of the argument

question: Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'Although most people are careful with data such as online banking details, many actually prefer more personalised marketing.' This sentence shows ________.
choices: that people are careless with all their data
a contrast between careful behaviour and preference for personalisation
that all people prefer personalised marketing

question: Read: 'There is no risk of the bathroom ________.' Which grammatical form correctly completes this sentence to mean 'flooding'?
Read: 'Good cookies make websites more user-friendly. ________, some are designed to steal private information.' (however)
choices: However, some are designed to steal private information
Although some are designed to steal private information
Despite some are designed to steal private information
In spite of some being designed to steal private information

question: According to the Life Skills matching exercise, Tip 3 'The same rules apply online as in real life' is matched with: ________.
choices: Take steps to hide anything negative
Ask yourself if you would be comfortable with your grandmother reading it
Do a search on your name every six months

question: According to the Life Skills section on social media, users often feel others are having a better time because people tend not to post about ________.
According to the Life Skills section on social media, some phones have a setting where you can set a ________ for your social media use.
In the passive advert exercise, complete: 'Our tech engineers ________ this state-of-the-art device.' (design) — This is active voice.
In the smart doorbell advert exercise, complete: 'To activate this function, the doorbell needs ________ with one of our smart locks.' (pair)
In the passive exercises, 'Celebrities' smartphones ________ regularly hacked into.' (past continuous passive)
In the impersonal passive exercises, complete: 'Five people ________ to have been injured in yesterday's accident.' (believe)
Read: 'It is claimed that wi-fi signals are harmless to birds and insects.' Which impersonal passive pattern is this?
choices: Pattern c: subject + passive + perfect infinitive
Pattern a: it + passive + that clause

question: According to the Life Skills section on managing your online image, employers today are very likely to look out for people who are actively ________ with others in their field of work.
